# Plate Tectonics

Plate tectonics is the theory that Earth's outer shell is divided into large
plates that move over the mantle. The theory was developed in the 1960s,
building on Alfred Wegener's earlier idea of continental drift. Plates can move
apart at divergent boundaries, collide at convergent boundaries, or slide past
one another at transform boundaries. The San Andreas Fault is a transform
boundary. Most earthquakes and volcanoes occur along plate boundaries.
